plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-514 RP17.P00.M00.S01-RP17.P01.M02.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-514 bootstraps RP17 AI Governance as a descriptor-only program package: the full P00 Contract And Acceptance Baseline phase (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, Primary Implementation Slice, Secondary Workflow Slice, Permission And Audit Binding, Synthetic Fixture Set, Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles) and the P01 head (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, and the Type And Shape Definition head) as fourteen descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-515 handoff, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-514 bootstraps the RP17 "AI Governance" program as a descriptor-only scope/contract foundation pack (150 units, RP17.P00.M00.S01-RP17.P01.M02.S08, risk class C). Independent read-only inspection supports the pack-level claims. I counted the 150 baseline units directly: deliverable distribution (implementation 108, contract 8, security_audit 16, hermes_evidence 7, claude_review 3, ui 8) and phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts match the prompt, contract cp514_requirements, registry constants, and manifest exactly. The product code (packages/ai-governance/src/*) is genuinely descriptor-only: pure functions returning Object.freeze'd descriptors with exhaustive no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data attestations; there are no database, network, object-storage, or filesystem-write operations in product paths (the test/validator read repo files only, which is tooling, not product runtime). The production_ready claim is correctly NOT asserted: command-evidence.json and manifest.json both report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status implemented_pending_review/pending_final_closeout — gates are not weakened. Claude is held as a read-only, non-final reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false) and enterprise trust is explicitly NOT claimed from local validation (human final approval required for runtime opening). Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; not embedded in product runtime). CP178+ review-hardening preconditions hold: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte valid-JSON stdout, non-zero), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used (not summary-only), prompt within size limit. Contract<->code<->test deep-equality is enforced and historical_pack_bindings ends at CP00-514. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this review stage, and unrelated dirty files are properly segregated from the pack scope. Two P3 (non-blocking) findings noted: (1) several forward-looking validator guards/ROW_EXTRAS keys are unexercised in this pack, and (2) the review-harness field enterprise_trust_claim_closed is ambiguously named but, on inspection, does not represent an affirmative enterprise-trust grant. Neither blocks pack or goal closeout. No P0/P1 issues. Final human approval remains required; Claude is not the final approver.

findings.title: Unexercised forward-looking validator guards and ROW_EXTRAS keys
findings.details: AI_GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP514_ROW_EXTRAS defines keys validation_helper, fixture_model, serialization_shape, model_unit_test, invalid_reference_test, ownership_drift_test, hermes_model_summary, and claude_model_review_prompt (service.js lines 100-108), and validators.js adds corresponding guard branches (lines 136-147). No row title in required_section_rows maps to these keys (confirmed: zero occurrences of 'Model unit test'/'Fixture model'/'Hermes model summary'/'Claude model review prompt' etc. in the contract), so these case-set fields are never populated and the guard branches always evaluate false in CP00-514. This is harmless dead/forward-looking scaffolding, almost certainly anticipating row titles introduced by later RP17 micro phases (Synthetic Fixture Set, Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet). It does not affect coverage, the descriptor-only boundary, or the production_ready posture.
findings.recommendation: Either add a brief comment marking these as forward-looking guards for downstream RP17 packs, or defer their introduction to the pack that first emits the corresponding rows so every guard is exercised by a test. Non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as non-blocking intentional scaffolding; no action required for CP00-514 closeout.

findings.title: Ambiguous 'enterprise_trust_claim_closed' field naming in review scaffolding
findings.details: The authority_boundary block (mirrored in prompt.txt) contains both human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim=true and enterprise_trust_claim_closed=true. Read in isolation, enterprise_trust_claim_closed=true could be misread as this pack/review affirmatively closing (granting) the enterprise trust claim, which would conflict with the human-approval requirement and the standing directive never to weaken production_ready/trust gates. I verified this is NOT the case: review-packet.md disambiguates it as 'Human/final approval and enterprise trust claim: closed outside this receipt', and the product attestations consistently state enterprise trust is explicitly NOT claimed from local validation (manifest enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation=true; registry claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; descriptor authority_boundary local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true). The intended meaning is 'handled/settled outside this automated receipt (reserved to humans)', not an affirmative grant.
findings.recommendation: Rename the harness field to something unambiguous (e.g., enterprise_trust_claim_handled_outside_receipt or enterprise_trust_claim_out_of_scope_for_receipt) to prevent a future reader from misreading the raw JSON as an enterprise-trust grant. Documentation/clarity only; not a gate violation.
findings.post_review_disposition: Verified not a gate violation; naming-clarity suggestion only, non-blocking for CP00-514 closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-514 ai governance core scope contract foundation descriptor pack
